symptoms.symptom_name: Sharp, brief, non-exertional left-sided chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Atypical non-anginal chest pain is characteristic of MVP syndrome dysautonomia.
Sharp, brief, non-exertional pain is atypical for ischaemia, though it does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations / awareness of heartbeat, worse lying quietly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpitations from benign ectopy are common in MVP syndrome.
Palpitations warrant an ECG/Holter to exclude a paroxysmal arrhythmia.
Panic-like symptoms are prominent, but must not become premature closure before cardiac exclusion.
Autonomic symptoms accompany MVP syndrome but are non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised fatigue and mild exercise intolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific fatigue reported in MVP syndrome; does not discriminate.

symptoms.symptom_name: No syncope / no loss of consciousness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of syncope lowers, but does not eliminate, the probability of a malignant arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: No radiation to jaw/arm and no exertional trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-radiating, non-exertional pain is strongly atypical for ischaemia, though an ECG is still required to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mid-systolic click and late systolic murmur that change dynamically with standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The dynamic mid-systolic click with late systolic murmur is the auscultatory hallmark of MVP — clinician-only, sealed, and not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Systolic mitral leaflet displacement on echocardiogram

investigation_details.expected_result: Systolic displacement of mitral leaflet(s) into the left atrium confirming prolapse; quantify any mitral regurgitation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of prolapse and regurgitation not documented; benign nature not formally confirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echocardiographic leaflet displacement is the confirmatory test — a hospital/clinic investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG to exclude ischaemia and characterise rhythm

investigation_details.expected_result: Anticipated normal / no acute ischaemic changes; may show benign ectopy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Acute coronary syndrome and arrhythmia not objectively excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal ECG is required to objectively lower the probability of ischaemia — cannot be done over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (BP 115/75, HR 82, SpO2 100%, afebrile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal clinician-measured observations are reassuring but non-specific; sealed as they were measured in person, not patient-obtained.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The mid-systolic click, late systolic murmur, dynamic change with standing and echocardiographic leaflet displacement together are diagnostic of MVP — and are precisely the clinician-only findings a telehealth chat cannot access.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Same-day objective exclusion of acute ischaemia and characterisation of rhythm before benign causes are accepted.
Non-urgent GP-referred confirmation of prolapse and quantification of any mitral regurgitation.
If palpitations recur, ambulatory monitoring characterises the rhythm during symptoms.
Not indicated for brief, non-exertional, low-risk atypical pain with no red flags; over-investigation would reinforce health anxiety.
